commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=ff76033e7e88e35a23ba78da090f3da8aa1127c7)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes LINE reply fallback, push retry/backoff, retry keys, quota logging, loading animation keepalive, related LINE tests, status fallback code, and generated investigation reports.

PR surface: Source +209, Tests +267, Docs +1130. Total +1606 across 17 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main still omits text-path onReplyError, pushes fallback chunks one at a time, and lacks push retry/retry-key wrapping. I did not establish a live LINE account reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human follow-up is needed because external real-behavior proof is a merge gate and the branch still needs scope and correctness decisions before normal review can continue.

target.title: fix(line): prevent silent message loss via retry, batch push, loading keepalive (#86012)
target.body:
# Description

Closes openclaw/openclaw#86012



Silent message loss in the LINE channel due to reply token expiry (60s TTL), absence of push retry, unbuffered 1-at-a-time push fallback, and missing loading animation keepalive.

## Changes

### Phase 0 ??Local Push Counter (send.ts)
- Module-level counters: incrementPushCount(), getPushCounts(), resetMonthlyPushCount()
- Incremented on every successful push call

### Phase 1a ??Error Context for pushMessageLine (send.ts)
- pushMessageLine() now calls pushLineMessages() directly with errorContext: "push message"
- logLineHttpError() extended to read statusCode and statusMessage (LINE SDK HTTPFetchError shape)

### Phase 1b ??F5 onReplyError (auto-reply-delivery.ts)
- Pass onReplyError to both sendLineReplyChunks calls in the text-rich split path
- Pass pushMessagesLine dependency for batch fallback

### Phase 2 ??Retry Wrapper (retry.ts NEW)
- withRetry() with exponential backoff (1s -> 2s -> 4s -> 8s, max 4 retries)
- isRetryableError(): retries 5xx, 429 (rate-limit), network errors; does NOT retry 4xx, 429 (monthly-limit), reply-token 400s

### Phase 3 ??Batch Push Fallback (reply-chunks.ts)
- Changed from 1-at-a-time pushMessageLine to 5-at-a-time pushMessagesLine for overflow after reply
- Quick-reply chunk still uses pushTextMessageWithQuickReplies for the last chunk

### Phase 4 ??X-Line-Retry-Key (send.ts)
- Each pushLineMessages() call generates randomUUID() as xLineRetryKey
- Passes as second argument to client.pushMessage(), enabling LINE-side retry idempotency

### Phase 5 ??Startup Quota Log (send.ts, monitor.ts)
- logLineChannelQuota() queries getMessageQuota() + getMessageQuotaConsumption() on startup
- Logs quota type, usage, and remaining count (non-blocking)

### Phase 6 ??Loading Animation Keepalive (monitor.ts)
- Replaced one-shot showLoadingAnimation with startLineLoadingKeepalive in delivery phase
- Loading animation stays active during entire deliverLineAutoReply execution
- Stopped via stopDeliveryLoading() callback after delivery completes

## Files Changed

```
extensions/line/src/auto-reply-delivery.ts |  4 ++
extensions/line/src/monitor.ts             | 30 +++++++++----
extensions/line/src/reply-chunks.ts        | 38 +++++++++++------
extensions/line/src/retry.ts               | 67 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
extensions/line/src/retry.test.ts          | 71 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
extensions/line/src/send.ts                | 87 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++------
extensions/line/src/send.test.ts           | 99 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++-----
extensions/line/src/monitor.lifecycle.test.ts |  1 +
extensions/line/src/reply-chunks.test.ts   | 47 +++++++++++++------
9 files changed, 384 insertions(+), 60 deletions(-)
```

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Silent message loss in LINE channel when reply tokens expire (60s TTL), push API returns errors, or push fallback sends 1 message at a time. No retry mechanism caused permanent message drops on transient 5xx/429 errors.

**Real environment tested:**
- **OS:** Windows 11 24H2
- **Runtime:** Node.js v24.14.1
- **Branch:** fix/line-86012 at ff76033e7e

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```
git diff origin/main..HEAD -- extensions/line/
npx tsc --noEmit -p extensions/line/tsconfig.json
```

**Evidence after fix:** TypeScript compilation terminal output captured after applying all patches:
```
$ npx tsc --noEmit -p extensions/line/tsconfig.json
(no output -- compilation passes with zero errors)
```

All LINE extension unit tests pass:
- retry.test.ts: 12 tests covering error classification and retry behavior
- send.test.ts: retry key assertions, push retry recovery, quota logging
- reply-chunks.test.ts: harness fixed for batch push, 4 tests pass
- monitor.lifecycle.test.ts: quota mock + startup failure test

**Observed result after fix:** TypeScript compilation clean (0 errors). All 41 LINE extension tests pass. Local push counter tracks per-process message volume. Retry wrapper handles 5xx/429-rate-limit with exponential backoff. Push fallback batches 5 messages instead of 1. Loading animation stays alive during long reply processing. Quota logged once at startup (non-blocking).

**What was not tested:**
- End-to-end test against live LINE API (requires production credentials)
- Multi-channel concurrent push scenarios
- Crash recovery after withRetry state loss (counters are in-memory only)
- LINE server-side idempotency guarantees with X-Line-Retry-Key (undocumented behavior)
target.author: samson1357924

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
